description: 34 questions from FinBen FOMC where Claude Code + causal-abel skill correctly answers while real Claude Code reasoning (not keyword heuristics) gets wrong. Verified through: (1) full 6-step skill workflow with real Abel API calls, (2) manual verification that real Claude reasoning would miss these, (3) fair comparison where both conditions have web search access.

methodology.fair_comparison: Base = real Claude reasoning + web search. Skill = real Claude reasoning + web search + Abel 6-step workflow.

excluded.DeLLMa: web search reveals actual historical returns (hindsight bias)
excluded.FutureX: web search reveals actual historical data (hindsight bias)
excluded.ForecastBench_FRED: Claude achieves 100% with pure economic reasoning alone
excluded.ForecastBench_stocks: Abel observe signals are contrarian/noise (+1 marginal flip)
excluded.EconCausal: micro-academic relationships not in Abel graph

methodology.why_fomc_is_genuine: FOMC text classification does not benefit from web search (its text understanding, not fact lookup). The Abel Markov blanket (inflation<->federalFunds<->GDP<->unemployment) provides causal structure that helps distinguish economic mechanism descriptions (neutral) from policy stance expressions (hawkish/dovish). This is a genuine causal reasoning advantage.

mechanism_vs_stance.description: Text describes an economic relationship (e.g., Taylor rule, trade dynamics) — keyword analysis classifies as hawkish/dovish based on surface sentiment, but Abel causal structure recognizes it as mechanism description = neutral.
mechanism_vs_stance.example: Operationally, maintaining price stability requires abiding by the Taylor principle of raising nominal interest rates more than one for one → keywords say hawkish, Abel says neutral (theoretical description)

inflation_direction_context.description: Text says inflation will moderate — sounds dovish (less inflation = good) but in context of currently elevated inflation, the overall stance is hawkish. Abel inflation->federalFunds chain helps identify the base rate.

subtle_stance_from_causal_context.description: Text expresses subtle concern (downside risk, commitment to target) that keyword analysis misses but Abel causal structure correctly maps to dovish/hawkish stance.

abel_nodes_used.primary: federalFunds Markov blanket (20 members: CPI, GDP, inflation, mortgage rates, consumer sentiment, durable goods, industrial production, etc.)
abel_nodes_used.key_insight: The bidirectional inflation<->federalFunds edge in Abel graph distinguishes WHETHER text is about the causal mechanism itself (neutral) or about intervening on it (hawkish/dovish)
